deficit_span.speaker: teacher
deficit_span.verbatim_quote: too much work you re not ready for middle school yet interesting

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: right i can give you more white paper cause it looks like you re doing your work on here which is fine you did it for each one so why did i give you this paper to continue the game

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: i wanted you what directions did i give i said next to each one put an explanation of how you know which one s larger so it looks like you don t have any room i gave another direction what were you supposed to do can everybody freeze and put their hands on their head pencils down what direction did i give on top of the directions that were already there

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: too much work you re not ready for middle school yet interesting let s look at 3 how do we say this decimal

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: 75 1000 how do we say this one

scenes.rationale: In response to a student asking whether they have to complete more work, the teacher characterizes the student's reluctance as evidence that the student is 'not ready for middle school,' attributing a deficit to the student's disposition/readiness rather than addressing the task or instruction.
